title: clinopodium nepeta subsp. bellantianum
intro: Clinopodium nepeta subsp. bellantianum, also known as catmint, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing, digestion, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving headaches and anxiety, and treating digestive problems such as bloating and gas. Clinopodium nepeta subsp. bellantianum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried herb, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
